sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Alexis, is a 30-year-old woman who presents with a 6-month history of numbness and tingling in her right hand, particularly in her thumb, index finger, and middle finger.
She reports experiencing pain when typing on a keyboard and using a mouse.
The symptoms have been more persistent over the last month.
She has been taking ibuprofen for pain relief and has found that rest and not typing on the keyboard also helps alleviate the symptoms.
She denies any pain at night and states that the symptoms are not affecting her sleep.
Past medical history: Not mentioned.
Review of systems: Not mentioned.
Current medications: I prescribed ibuprofen 600 milligrams four times a day for pain management.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has pain on extension and flexion of the wrist.
She has a positive Tinel's sign on the inside of her wrist and forearm.
She has decreased grip strength on the right hand.
She reports numbness and tingling in her thumb, index finger, and middle finger.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I suspect carpal tunnel syndrome based on the patient's symptoms and physical examination findings.
Problem list: Carpal tunnel syndrome; active.
Plan:
I will put the patient in a wrist splint to wear at night.
I will continue to monitor her symptoms and consider ordering physical therapy or surgery if necessary, pending the results of the nerve conduction studies.
I will order an EMG (nerve conduction study) to confirm the diagnosis.
Follow-up: I will review the results of the EMG study and discuss further treatment options with the patient at her next visit.
